message:
test(fuzz): add host_invoke_dispatch fuzz target (resolves br-jdef7)

Drives an adversarial InvokeRequest + ZonePolicyObject through the two
boundaries the host gateway crosses for every /rpc/invoke:

1. CapabilityVerifier::without_instance_binding + verify_unbound — the
   gateway-vantage capability check (br-5qp7o).
2. simulate_policy_decision — the zone-policy decision shipped via
   fcp-core::policy.

The fuzzer constructs a real signed CapabilityToken using a fixed
Ed25519 key, then perturbs token zone vs request zone vs policy zone,
required-capability vs token-capability, required-operation vs
token-operation, expiration mode (valid / expired / not-yet-valid), and
zone policy principal_allow / principal_deny patterns.

Invariants pinned across every input:
- Neither verify_unbound nor simulate_policy_decision panics.
- verify_unbound returning Ok implies the produced UnboundVerified
  token's claims() accessor is panic-free AND token zone == request
  zone (zone-binding contract).
- simulate_policy_decision Ok must echo the request zone in the receipt
  header.
- ZoneMismatch only surfaces when request zone actually differs from
  policy zone (reverse direction of the contract).
- All PolicySimulationError variants are exhaustively covered (a new
  variant added without updating the fuzz target fails the match-arm
  exhaustiveness check at compile time).
- Re-running simulate_policy_decision with the same input yields the
  same Decision discriminant (determinism).

Build verified: cargo build --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin
fuzz_host_invoke_dispatch (4m02s, clean).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -349,6 +349,13 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_host_invoke_dispatch"
+path = "fuzz_targets/host_invoke_dispatch.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "graphql_ws_message"
 path = "fuzz_targets/graphql_ws_message.rs"
